message:
fix(wasm): treat unsafe-integer-range floats with no fractional part as REAL instead of erroring

Change parse_js_number_value to treat JavaScript numbers that are outside
Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER range but have no fractional part (e.g. 1e20) as
REAL values rather than returning a TypeMismatch error. This aligns with
the REAL <-> number type mapping spec: if the value can't safely be
represented as an i64 integer, store it as a float rather than rejecting
it outright. Values within the safe integer range continue to be stored
as INTEGER as before.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
